consolidated_diary:
[S1901M] No negotiations have taken place yet in this phase.
Intent: Expand aggressively into contested territories. Focus on securing the north (St. Petersburg/Murman) and preparing for expansion into Scandinavia or the Black Sea/Galicia regions. I will assign orders to all units to ensure maximum pressure.

[S1901M] The opening phase was marked by significant friction in the East. My attempt to seize Ukraine was thwarted by a bounce between my armies in Moscow and Warsaw, leaving my expansion into the south stalled. While I successfully moved the fleet from Sevastopol to Romania, the failure to secure Ukraine is a setback for my goal of rapid expansion. Meanwhile, Turkey has moved aggressively, securing Bulgaria and Syria, which signals they are positioning themselves for dominance in the Black Sea and Levant. Germany's movement toward Holland and the Ruhr suggests they are consolidating central Europe. I must reassess my approach to Ukraine; perhaps a more coordinated effort or a diplomatic overture to a neighbor is needed to prevent such stalemates. My priority remains securing the north and breaking the deadlock in the south to ensure my supply centers grow.

[F1901M] No negotiations have occurred in this phase yet.
Intent: Execute aggressive expansion. I will attempt to resolve the Ukraine deadlock by coordinating Moscow and Warsaw movements to avoid bounces. I will also position the fleet in St. Petersburg/Murman for Scandinavian expansion and monitor Turkey's presence in Bulgaria to secure the Black Sea.

[F1901M] The expansion into the south has finally yielded results. By coordinating the movements of Moscow and Warsaw, I successfully secured Ukraine and Galicia, breaking the previous deadlock and increasing my supply center count. The fleet in the Black Sea is now positioned to monitor Turkish aggression, which is vital given their successful expansion into Syria and Armenia. However, Turkey's presence in the Black Sea remains a significant threat to my southern flank. In the north, my fleet in St. Petersburg remains secure, providing a stable base for future Scandinavian operations. While I have gained momentum, the board is becoming increasingly crowded. My next priority is to solidify these gains and prevent Turkey from turning the Black Sea into a Turkish lake. I must also keep a close eye on Germany's rapid consolidation in the west to ensure they do not become an unstoppable force.
final_goals: Reclaim Sevastopol and secure the Black Sea from Turkish expansion.
Consolidate control over Ukraine and Galicia to build a stable southern front.
Prepare a naval expedition from St. Petersburg to contest Scandinavia.
Monitor German movements in Belgium and Prussia to prevent a western encirclement.
